title: Intercity personnel exchange is more effective than policy transplantation at reducing water pollution

data_scope.availability: core firm/investment/patent data under NDA (onsite at PKU); city-leader and compiled socioeconomic data open on GitHub; code on GitHub

core_conclusion: Moving people beats copying policies: intercity exchange of Chinese city leaders cut firm-level water pollution by 4.78-15.26% (2006-2013), contributing 39.45-57.98% of national industrial water-pollution reduction, while 'policy transplantation only' was insignificant or even harmful. Exchanged leaders carry tacit governance experience, broker intercity green investment and patent collaboration, and launch pro-green industrial policies; interprovincial and first-mover-to-late-comer flows give the largest, inequality-reducing effects.
why_it_matters: Reframes the 'copy successful policies' prescription for lagging regions by showing tacit, person-bound governance knowledge is what actually transfers; provides the first nationwide firm-level causal estimate of political-leader mobility on pollution; acts as an equity instrument that narrows spatial governance disparities; works through process improvement rather than end-of-pipe abatement.
why_nature: Transferable governance principle ('move knowledge with people, not paper') relevant to green transition globally; couples human/political systems to firm emissions and spatial inequality; causally credible at national scale via quasi-random rotation and staggered DID with placebo tests; strongly policy-translatable; integrates rare multi-source micro-data (CVs, firm pollution/economics, patents, investment, policy texts).
added_value_to_my_research: Leader-mobility graph + community detection -> identify health-policy knowledge brokers
LLM/embedding policy model -> measure genuine localization of telehealth/accessibility policies
Causal-forest CATE + SHAP -> where new clinics/charging stations buy the most equity
Cadre reassignment as constrained optimization -> portable to health-workforce/EV allocation
Emission-to-exposure-to-health chain -> MGWR + remote-sensing test of environmental-health equity

figures.caption: Network of intercity city-leader exchanges in China, 2006-2013 (line width proportional to number of exchanged leaders)
Effect of leader exchange on firm-level COD, ammonia nitrogen, wastewater and its 39.45-57.98% share of national reduction
Personnel exchange vs policy transplantation: transplantation-only insignificant/harmful, exchange+transplantation strongest
Governance-experience mechanism: environment-department background and first-mover-to-late-comer flows reduce pollution more
Cooperation mechanism: intercity green/high-tech investment, patent collaboration (extensive margin) and green-industry tax cuts
Motivation: exchanged leaders are younger and respond more to difficult central-government reduction targets
